title: prunus × lannesiana f. appendiculata
intro: Prunus × lannesiana f. appendiculata, also known as the Chinese cherry or plum, is a deciduous shrub or small tree that belongs to the rose family (Rosaceae) and is a hybrid of Prunus lannesiana and Prunus tomentosa. This plant has been used in traditional Chinese medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antioxidant, and antiviral activities. The health benefits of Prunus × lannesiana f. appendiculata include reducing fever, alleviating coughs and sore throats, and protecting against cardiovascular diseases due to its high levels of flavonoids and anthocyanins. In herbal preparations, the plant is often used to make infusions, decoctions, and tinctures, which are used to treat various health conditions, including respiratory infections and digestive problems.
